## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. Today we're going to talk about how you can increase your budget slowly and safely. Let's go! Here's how to increase your ads budget without screwing up results. Facebook provides pathetic guidance on this. They say that if you increase your budget from $100 to $101, which nobody will do, it isn't likely to restart the learning phase. If you increase from $100 to $1000, it might. Yeah, no kidding. Really not helpful. What about everything in between? So here's what I recommend you do in this situation. Create an automated rule. If your results for the prior day were within an acceptable range, have your budget automatically increased by a very small amount, just like 1%. You can have this process run every night. As long as you continue to get good results, your budget will increase. But slowly, these slow increases shouldn't restart the learning phase. But this is a good way, especially for a long-term campaign, to leverage something that's working without screwing up your results. Hopefully, of course, the algorithm's crazy. Follow for more.
